name: TMC the metals Co Inc.
business: TMC is a deep seabed minerals developer focused on the collection, processing, and refining of polymetallic nodules found on the seafloor in international waters of the Clarion Clipperton Zone. The nodules contain high concentrations of nickel, copper, cobalt, and manganese, which are critical metals used in strategic sectors including semiconductors, AI data centers, steel manufacturing, defense, and marine industrial base. The company plans to produce nickel-, cobalt-, and copper-bearing intermediate and metal cathode products, as well as a manganese silicate product, and may expand into silicomanganese alloy, battery-grade sulfates, precursor Cathode Active Materials, and rare earth elements.

exposures.sentence: The four metals and REEs contained in the polymetallic nodules are on the U.S. Department of Interior's 2025 List of Critical Minerals, with end-uses in strategic sectors including semiconductors and AI data centers, steel manufacturing, the defense and marine industrial base.

The four metals and REEs contained in the polymetallic nodules are on the U.S. Department of Interior's 2025 List of Critical Minerals, with end-uses in strategic sectors including semiconductors and AI data centers, steel manufacturing, the defense and marine industrial base.
Copper, nickel, cobalt and manganese are integral to semiconductor devices (e.g., chips, transistors) and the equipment used to manufacture them (e.g., deposition tools, etching systems).
Copper enables efficient electrical grids, transformers, and wiring for new power plant integration and transmission.

Nickel, cobalt, and manganese are essential for high-energy-density lithium-ion batteries that boost range and stability in electric vehicles (EVs) and other battery-powered applications like drones and robots.

Nickel and cobalt are critical for high-temperature superalloys in jet engines and turbine blades, enabling superior heat resistance and performance in aircraft and missiles; manganese strengthens structural steels for airframes and armor; copper provides essential conductivity in avionics wiring and electrical systems, supporting radar and communication equipment.

Manganese is a key alloying element for deoxidizing, desulfurizing, and strengthening steel, making it tougher, more impact-resistant, and essential in low-carbon and high-strength steels (up to 1.8% content in specialized varieties used in pipelines and transportation).

Nickel, cobalt, and manganese are essential for high-energy-density lithium-ion batteries that boost range and stability in electric vehicles (EVs) and other battery-powered applications like drones and robots.
Polymetallic nodules ... have high concentrations of nickel, copper, cobalt and manganese, alongside meaningful concentrations of rare earth elements (REE) in a single rock.
Nickel and cobalt are critical for high-temperature superalloys in jet engines and turbine blades, enabling superior heat resistance and performance in aircraft and missiles.
Nickel and cobalt form superalloys for high-efficiency generators and turbines in ... nuclear reactors (e.g., for control rods and vessel linings resistant to radiation and high temperatures).
Nickel and cobalt form superalloys for high-efficiency generators and turbines in hydroelectric, solar and nuclear reactors ... and geothermal systems.

catalysts.event: Release of NORI-D PFS declaring world's first mineral reserves for a seafloor polymetallic nodule project
catalysts.sentence: On August 4, 2025, we released (i) the NORI-D PFS, which declared the world's first mineral reserves for a seafloor polymetallic nodule project demonstrating the project's economic viability.

catalysts.event: TMC USA submitted first-ever application for commercial recovery permit and two exploration licenses under DSHMRA
catalysts.sentence: On April 29, 2025, we announced that TMC USA had submitted the first-ever application for a commercial recovery permit and two exploration licenses under DSHMRA.

catalysts.event: NOAA confirmed full compliance of TMC USA's exploration license applications
catalysts.sentence: On August 11, 2025, TMC USA received notice of full compliance from NOAA on its exploration applications, and confirmation that TMC USA has priority right over both exploration areas.

catalysts.event: Successful production of battery-grade high-purity manganese sulfate from nodule-derived manganese silicate
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, we announced that we had successfully produced battery-grade, high-purity manganese sulfate from our nodule-derived manganese silicate product during bench scale trials at our partner KPM's operating facility in Ontario.

catalysts.event: PAMCO successfully smelted 450 tonnes of calcine into NiCuCo alloy and Mn silicate products
catalysts.sentence: On February 18, 2025, we announced that PAMCO had successfully smelted 450 tonnes of calcine into 35 tonnes of NiCuCo alloy and 320 tonnes of Mn silicate products.

catalysts.event: U.S. Executive Order signed to expedite permitting and evaluate offtake of critical minerals from nodules
catalysts.sentence: On April 25, 2025, we welcomed an Executive Order signed by President Trump to create a robust domestic supply for critical minerals derived from seabed resources.

catalysts.event: Revised Sponsorship Agreement with Nauru signed
catalysts.sentence: On June 4, 2025, we announced the signing of a revised Sponsorship Agreement (Agreement), updating the terms of the Agreement signed between the parties in 2017.

catalysts.event: Revised Sponsorship Agreement with Tonga signed
catalysts.sentence: On August 4, 2025, we announced the signing of a revised Sponsorship Agreement (Agreement), updating the terms of the Agreement signed between the parties in 2021.

catalysts.event: Rutger Bosland joined TMC as Chief Innovation and Offshore Technology Officer
catalysts.sentence: On April 15, 2025, we announced that Rutger Bosland, the engineer and technical lead who oversaw the design, build, and successful test deployment of Allseas integrated nodule collection system, had joined the Company as Chief Innovation and Offshore Technology Officer (CIOTO).

On June 16, 2025, we announced that Korea Zinc, a world leader in non-ferrous metal refining and precursor Cathode Active Material (pCAM) technology, had agreed to make a strategic investment of approximately $85.2 million in us through the purchase of common shares and warrants in a private placement.

catalysts.event: TMC USA filed first consolidated deep-seabed mining application under NOAA's new process
catalysts.sentence: On January 22, 2026, we announced that TMC USA had submitted a consolidated application to NOAA for an exploration license and a commercial recovery permit for polymetallic nodules in international waters of the CCZ.

catalysts.event: NOAA determined TMC USA's consolidated application is in substantial compliance
catalysts.sentence: On March 9, 2026, we announced that NOAA determined that the consolidated application submitted by TMC USA for an exploration license and commercial recovery permit is in substantial compliance with the applicable requirements under DSHMRA and its implementing regulations.

catalysts.event: Strategic Partnership Agreement with Mariana Minerals signed
catalysts.sentence: On March 19, 2026, we signed a Strategic Partnership Agreement with Mariana Minerals (Mariana) focusing on the potential development of a nodule processing and refining facility in the Port of Brownsville, Texas as part of our owner's team.

catalysts.event: Exclusive right of negotiation with Port of Brownsville for lease option
catalysts.sentence: On March 27, 2026, we announced that TMC USA currently holds an exclusive right of negotiation with the Port of Brownsville, Texas on a lease and / or lease option for land sufficient to develop a domestic nodule processing and refining ecosystem.

tamClaims.claim: Global battery market size expected to be 4.2 TWh in 2030 and 6.8 TWh by 2035, with more than 85% demand from lithium-ion batteries.
tamClaims.sentence: McKinsey Battery Insights expects a global battery market size of 4.2 TWh in 2030 and 6.8 TWh by 2035, with more than 85% of this demand driven by lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) primarily due to demand growth for battery electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage, but with increasing demand expected from drones and robots.

tamClaims.claim: Data centers copper demand projected to increase from 440,000 tonnes in 2025 to around 690,000 tonnes by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: data centers are poised to become one of the most mineral-intensive components of the digital economy, increasing from 440,000 tonnes in 2025 to around 690,000 tonnes of copper by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: AI-driven data centers projected to consume up to 500,000 tonnes of copper annually by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: AI-driven data centers projected to consume up to 500,000 tonnes annually by 2030 due to their electricity intensity and thermal management needs.

tamClaims.claim: Global steel production projected to grow by 8% through 2030 to 2 billion tonnes.
tamClaims.sentence: Global steel production is projected to grow by 8% through 2030, to 2 billion tonnes;

tamClaims.claim: Steel demand in the United States projected to grow by 40% by 2035.
tamClaims.sentence: It is projected that demand for steel in the United States will grow by 40% by 2035.

tamClaims.claim: Copper supply gap projected to worsen to 8 million tonnes by 2035 and 10 million tonnes by 2040.
tamClaims.sentence: In the case of copper, supply shortages have already commenced and are projected to worsen to 8 million tonnes by 2035, and 10 million tonnes by 2040.
